title: Crocodeilanthe spiralis
intro: Crocodeilanthe spiralis, also known as the spiral crocodile's tail, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crocodeilanthe spiralis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crocodeilanthe spiralis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and bronchitis.
